headline: Rural Road Maintenance Backlog Reaches RM14 Billion
context: JKR estimated maintenance backlog for 65,000km rural road network at RM14 billion as of December 2025.

cards.big: RM14 billion maintenance backlog for 65,000km of rural roads
cards.sub: Pothole repairs alone cost RM1.2 billion annually addressing only 40% of damage

cards.big: 45% of rural roads rated below acceptable condition by JKR assessment
cards.sub: Poor surfaces contribute to 2,800 accidents annually on rural routes

cards.big: Annual maintenance budget of RM2.8 billion covers only 20% of actual needs
cards.sub: At current funding backlog would take 7 years if no further deterioration

cards.big: Climate-related road damage increased 35% between 2020 and 2025
cards.sub: Flooding and landslides destroy repairs faster than maintenance crews work

cards.big: Road maintenance tests whether rural infrastructure receives sustained investment
RM14 billion rural road backlog. Maintenance debt compounds — delay today costs more tomorrow.
